content: Well, um, so, yeah, when I got invited to this dinner, I just felt like everyone was really curious about what I had to say. I kept smiling because I figured if I said something silly or different, they’d think I’m weird or incompetent. But then I started laughing at the jokes, even though I didn’t really find them funny. Honestly, I just wanted to fit in and pretend I wasn’t awkward, which made me seem less interesting. It’s exhausting trying to act like someone important, especially after a few times where I tried to push myself too much and failed. I guess I just feel like no matter what I try, I’m going to fail. It’s hard to connect with people who aren’t exactly my kind. I wonder if there’s anything wrong with me. It’s frustrating because I want to make friends and contribute to the team, but sometimes I just can’t stop worrying that I’ll embarrass myself or disappoint them. So, I guess I’m just stuck in a cycle of pretending I’m okay and liking people. I appreciate it when people accept me, but honestly, I wish I could shake things off and let them go. It’s exhausting. I guess I need to start thinking differently and maybe join in more conversations than usual to show I’m interested and willing to learn. I’m sorry if these answers sound fake or don’t match how XXX actually thinks. Please help me figure out why I keep falling into those situations again. I just need some guidance. Thank you!

Yeah, lately, my husband hasn’t been as much of a conversationalist either. It feels like he’s kind of avoiding me or maybe just wants to make me feel better when I’m away. I keep thinking, “Maybe they’re just tired or bored, which means I’ll never be able to keep up.” Or else, they might notice how quiet I’m being and freak out. So, I’ve ended up trying to comfort them or just pretending everything’s okay, but then I end up feeling lonely and maybe even worse than usual. It’s exhausting. I guess I just don’t know what to do. It feels like no matter what I do, I’m always going to fail. I wish there was some way to fix things, but honestly, I don’t really care if people see me as useless. I just want to be happy and comfortable, but sometimes I forget that I’m the one who needs to be loved. I appreciate it when they say they love me, but sometimes I just wish they’d stop bothering me. I guess I need someone to trust, but right now, I’m afraid they won’t like me anymore. I’m sorry I had to pretend this wasn’t happening. I just need to figure out how to connect again. Please let me know if I’m on the right path or if I’m just making this all harder. I really want to be loved, but I’m scared it’s too late.

Yeah, well, lately, I’ve just been really stuck on this project. It feels like no matter how hard I try, I keep slipping up again, even though I’m supposed to know everything. I keep thinking maybe I’ll mess things up because everyone seems so confident right now, and I’m afraid they’ll notice something wrong. So, instead of trying to get started, I just… wait until tomorrow and then just skip it. It’s exhausting and makes me feel ashamed, but honestly, I don’t want people to find out I’m not cut out for this kind of stuff. I guess I just need someone to tell me that I’m not good enough and help me figure out why.

Oh, yeah, this time I was really nervous at the party. I kept thinking everyone was whispering because they were trying to catch my eye or maybe just being silly. Even when I tried to join in, I found myself falling behind and getting too distracted. Then, after we talked some more, I just wanted to get away quickly because I didn’t want to be seen as weird or awkward. It’s hard to explain exactly why I feel this way, but sometimes I just feel like I’m invisible even though I’m there. I guess I’m scared I’ll embarrass myself if I’m not like everyone else. It’s frustrating because I want to connect but still feel out of place. I wish I could stop checking my phone and focus on what’s happening around me instead of dwelling on my own thoughts. I’ve been thinking about changing plans, but honestly, I’m scared I might ruin the experience again. I just hope they’ll let me go anyway.

Well, I guess I’ve been having a really tough time lately at work. When we talked about something like how I want to start planning my next big project, I felt like maybe it wasn’t going to go anywhere because then I’d just end up doing nothing or saying things that didn’t help. It’s frustrating because I know deep down I’m capable of thinking outside of the box, but every time I try to push back, it just makes me feel like I’m failing. It’s like I’m trapped in this loop of wanting change but not being able to get it. I don’t know why I’m stuck in this place where I feel so powerless and helpless. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I just wish someone could see how deeply trapped I am.

Yeah, this time it felt like everyone just didn’t care what I had to say. When they started talking about different ways to live their life, I felt like nothing really mattered anymore—like I was stuck somewhere invisible. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I just wanted to leave, but I kept thinking, “If I’m going to be a part of this, I’ll never make it.” I guess this pattern keeps coming back because whenever I try to push my limits, I just end up being pushed away instead. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to stop. I’ve tried asking them about their own lives, but it doesn’t change anything. So, I’ve just decided to stay quiet most days, which makes me feel worse than if I were lying on the couch all day. I don’t want to be around people, so it’s hard to keep up with things that interest me. It’s like I’m just not good enough to fit in here.

I went to this gathering because I’m afraid that if I talk too much or show some signs of initiative, people will notice something wrong. But when I started speaking up, instead of saying what I really wanted to say, I ended up just agreeing to things that didn’t make sense. I felt like I was drowning in this pressure and then replaying everything later, feeling like I had no choice but to conform. It’s exhausting to feel like I’m not making enough of an impact despite trying my hardest. Honestly, whenever I see other kids doing something new or taking risks, I feel even more alone and helpless. I guess I just don’t understand why they’re so demanding. It’s like I’m being judged by the same set of standards that they expect from me.

Oh, yeah, that argument was pretty intense. My partner kept saying things like “I don’t want to talk about this” because they were afraid I’d say something wrong or disagree with them. I’ve tried to stay quiet and even agreed with most things, but it felt like no matter what I said, they didn’t seem to care or wanted me to change my mind. It’s hard not to feel scared that if I try to express myself, they’ll just leave me. So, I just suppressed my feelings and pretended everything was okay. It’s like I’m trapped in a loop where I need to please them, then get rejected again. That’s why I keep agreeing to whatever they say anyway. It makes me feel worse than I already do. I guess I just don’t know how to stop being controlled by what I want. I wish there was a way to say no or ask for different ideas, but honestly, I’m too afraid of disappointing them. I guess I just need someone to listen and understand me better. But maybe that won’t work out the way I planned. I’m scared they’re going to lose interest or decide to move on. I’m sorry I’m making these situations harder for us. I appreciate you taking this approach. Please let me know if there’s anything else I can help explain or clarify. I need some real answers right away.

Yeah, the family gathering was kind of awkward. When I saw everyone chatting happily, I felt like maybe there were no ones around—maybe they didn’t even want me here. I kept thinking, “Why am I here? Why isn’t anyone there?” It’s like they’re not interested in what I have to say, or they just don’t care enough about me. I’ve tried to hide away from it, but it just makes me feel even worse. I don’t know why, but when I’m around people, I end up feeling like I’m not good enough for them. It’s like, if I’m not perfect, then they’ll never find out how much I love them. It’s exhausting to just sit there and wait for things to change.

Yeah, I was just checking my budget again this morning, and honestly, it’s like no matter how hard I try, I’m just not getting where I’m going. I kept thinking, “What if I don’t make enough money next month? It’s too much for me,” so I didn’t really look at any jobs anymore, and I ended up staying late studying because it seemed easier than starting something else. I’ve been avoiding looking for jobs or trying different ways to earn money because I’m scared of showing weakness, or maybe it’s just harder to push through things when I feel like I’m failing. It’s exhausting, and I’m just stuck in a cycle where nothing seems to change.

I’ve been thinking about applying for that promotion because it seems so important, but then I get these thoughts inside that say, “Everyone will see how bad I am.” It’s like no matter what I try, it’ll always show that I’m not cut out for this job. I keep putting off the application because I worry that if something goes wrong, everyone will recognize me as inadequate. It feels like a constant reminder of being too small, too insignificant, and failing to meet expectations. I’m afraid to talk to anyone because I’m scared of showing weakness or being judged. It’s frustrating because I want to prove myself but also want to avoid getting in trouble or losing my position. I guess I’m just avoiding facing those fears head-on because they make me feel worse than they already do. I need some guidance or maybe even a change of heart, but right now, I’m stuck in this endless cycle of fear.

Well, today was really tough starting on that project. I kept thinking, “This is too much for me, it’s going to be like no one can finish it.” I ended up postponing some things because I felt so overwhelmed, and then I just spent all afternoon worrying. It’s like I’m afraid to even start because I know I’ll mess everything up anyway. I guess there’s something about this project that makes me feel so inadequate, and it just keeps happening again. Honestly, I don’t want to talk to anyone about it because I don’t want to look silly or prove to myself that I’m not good enough. It’s frustrating because it feels like no matter what I try, I’m just going down the same road over and over. I guess I need someone to help me figure out what I’m supposed to do better, but it’s hard to trust myself when I’m already feeling so defeated.

Yeah, so last week at the meeting, it felt really overwhelming. I was trying to connect with some coworkers, but when someone said something funny or interesting, I just shut down. It made me feel like maybe I wasn’t good enough or interesting enough to be around them. After leaving, I just felt very quiet and then lost, like I couldn’t even start another conversation. It’s hard for me to keep going after that. Honestly, I guess I just want to fit in, but sometimes I’m afraid people will notice how different I am. I wish I could tell them what I think, but I don’t want to come across as weird or silly. It’s like I’m stuck in this never-ending loop where no matter what I say, I end up being dismissed. It’s exhausting to keep pretending I’m cool, but honestly, I just don’t feel comfortable being myself there.

Yeah, um, well, lately, when they say I’m spending too much time at work, I just feel like maybe I’m being too needy. It’s like, if I try to help out, they’ll figure out I’m just making things worse. So, I’ve canceled some plans because I’m afraid they’re going to notice I’m acting differently than usual. It’s exhausting to stay late because I’m worried if I really mess up, they’ll think less of me. Honestly, whenever I ask them what’s going on, they always talk about all these little things—like I’m just being too lazy or something. It makes me feel even worse about myself. I guess I just want to prove to them that I’m worth keeping around here. But then, they always come back with excuses saying I’m being a good partner anyway. It’s exhausting, but honestly, I need someone to give me some peace of mind that I’m still loved. I don’t know, I guess this whole idea of making everyone else happy seems easier than proving to myself that I’m truly valuable.

Yeah, so, um, I got that offer, and honestly, I just felt really anxious because I’m used to being on stage all the time. When I stood up there, though, I started thinking, “What if nobody notices what I’m saying? Or maybe they’ll just laugh off my idea.” So, I decided to go back home early and just get everything done. It’s hard because even though I know I’m probably wrong, I feel like I’m missing out on learning something new. And then, I’ve been working late almost every night since then, trying to make sure everything is perfect. It’s exhausting and makes me want to give up. But I don’t want to let anyone down—I really miss having control. It’s frustrating because I’m scared that if I mess up, they’ll see I’m not cut out for this job. I guess it’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where I keep feeling like I’m failing, but I can’t help it.

Yeah, during the dinner, my partner finally said I wanted to go somewhere different. I was hesitant at first because I didn’t want them to feel uncomfortable, but then I just felt this overwhelming pressure to agree. Honestly, I kept thinking, “If they don’t like what I say, maybe they won’t actually like me anyway.” So, instead of saying no, I just checked if anyone noticed anything odd about how I looked or acted. It made me feel even more anxious than usual, kind of like trying too hard to please someone makes me look stupid. I guess I’m scared that if I tell them what I really want, they’ll think less of me. It’s frustrating because it’s like I’m already failing at making myself comfortable. I’d rather pretend things were okay than face the truth.

Yeah, so lately at the family dinner, they kept talking about my career choices and how I wasn’t really contributing much. It felt like no matter what I said, it just didn’t get noticed. I ended up leaving early because I felt so overwhelmed and kind of stuck there, even though I wanted to say something. Honestly, I guess I just feel like maybe I’m failing them somehow. It’s hard to connect with people unless I’m doing something truly amazing myself, which makes me feel like I’m always going down the same road again. I don’t know, I just… don’t know where to start. It’s like I’m constantly falling apart, and I want to fix it, but I’m afraid I’ll mess things up again. It’s frustrating, but I’ve tried my best to stay out of trouble, but sometimes it’s easier to just leave anyway. So, I guess I’m just trying to figure out why I keep feeling this way and why I’m stuck here. It’s like I’m not sure what to expect next time.

Hey therapist, I’ve been feeling really overwhelmed lately because my partner kept suggesting we go somewhere different this holiday, which isn’t exactly what we planned. I keep thinking maybe they’re just tired of me being around, or maybe they just don’t want to deal with the same stressors I’m causing. So, I’m trying so hard to convince them to stay home, but it’s exhausting and I feel like maybe they’ll decide to move on. Honestly, it’s just making me more anxious and scared that if I don’t try harder, they’ll leave me again. It’s like I’m playing a game where no matter how much I try, I’m always failing. I just don’t know why I’m stuck here. I need some clues, but nothing seems to stick. Please, help me figure out what’s going on. I’m sorry if this feels disrespectful or off-topic. I appreciate you taking the time to understand me better. I love you, and I’m hoping you can see something inside me that’s driving me crazy.

Oh, hey therapist. So, I guess this week has really been tough at work. When I’m given a new project, I always find myself just thinking, “I’ll never get this right.” It’s like someone’s already figured out everything I need to know, so why bother trying? I keep putting off doing anything because I’m afraid if something goes wrong, people will see how stupid I am. Sometimes I just feel like I’m being judged even though there’s nothing wrong with what I’m doing. It’s exhausting, and I just want to escape this place where I feel like I’m invisible. I don’t know, I guess it’s all just one big weight hanging over me. Please let me know if I’m going too far or if I need help. I’d really appreciate it if you could look into this for me. I just don’t want anyone to see how bad things are getting.

Well, this dinner was really tough. When we started talking about everyone’s work, my parents just kept saying how great their new thing is, which made me feel like maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of success. After the dinner, I just felt so exhausted and like no one understood or cared about how frustrating things were going for me. I ended up leaving earlier than planned because it just wasn’t enough. It’s hard to face all that pressure when you’re constantly being compared to others. I guess it’s okay to feel this way sometimes, but honestly, I want to stop pretending I’m perfect. I wish I could change anything, but it feels like it always makes me worse off.

I’ve been so nervous lately because I’m worried that if I don’t come, everyone will notice how awkward I am and won’t want me there. It feels like no matter what I say, people just want to leave or laugh off my presence. I keep thinking that if I’m not careful, someone will realize I’m boring or unappealing. So, I ended up making an excuse because I didn’t want to go, and instead spent the night alone trying to forget about it. Honestly, it’s exhausting because when I finally get home, I feel even worse than when I first went. I guess I just don’t trust myself to handle things at work or in life. It’s like I’m always putting myself down and not being good enough, which makes me feel even more helpless.

Yeah, the dinner was pretty rough. My parents just kept talking about how my brother did better than I ever could. It made me feel like everyone else had a better life than mine. I guess I’m just so stuck in this never-changing pattern where I’m invisible to them because I don’t seem to fit in there either. Sometimes when I try to talk, I just end up crying or withdrawing because I know it won’t help anyone. I keep thinking maybe I’m flawed somehow, and that makes me feel even worse than before. It’s frustrating because I want to connect, but I’m afraid of making things worse. I guess I’m scared that if I show any signs of weakness, people will see me as unworthy and leave me alone. I’ll probably stay quiet later, replaying all these thoughts over and over, which just reinforces how deeply depressed I am. It’s hard to connect with others, especially since I’m trying to hide who I am behind a facade. I hope they can see that I’m struggling and that something needs to change. But right now, I’m just too stuck in this cycle of being invisible and feeling useless.

Yeah, I guess that whole idea of joining that community group just felt like a big burden. When I started talking there, I was worried that if I showed off anything, it might cause them to think less of me or that I’m somehow not cut out for this kind of stuff. So, I stayed pretty quiet most of the time, mostly because I didn’t want to risk getting rejected or making things worse. It’s like whenever I try to connect, it always ends up being awkward or confusing. I feel like maybe I’m stuck in a loop where I can’t really express myself enough. It’s exhausting to stay around, especially after feeling like everyone already knows everything.

Yeah, this past week was pretty tough. At the get-together, I just felt invisible because nobody seemed to care about me. When people started talking, I kept quiet because I didn’t want to seem awkward or useless. I ended up leaving early because I was just so overwhelmed and stuck in a rut. Sometimes I wonder if I’m meant to be something interesting, but then I realize maybe I never really fit in there at all. It’s exhausting, and I feel like I’m failing to prove myself. I guess I just can’t figure out how to connect with people or explain myself properly. It’s like, even though I know I need to talk, I keep getting caught up in my own thoughts and forget where I’m supposed to be.

Yeah, this whole project came up, and honestly, just thinking about it makes me feel really anxious. When I first mentioned it, I felt like I was being watched right through, so quickly checking every little thing made me double-check again. It’s exhausting trying to keep everything straight, and I’m scared people are already seeing I’m not cut out for this. Plus, I don’t want to come off as incompetent, especially since everyone seems to already think I’m not good enough. It’s like I’m always falling apart, and no matter how much I try, I end up failing. So, avoiding these things feels safer than facing them head-on. It’s exhausting, but I guess I’ve got to keep pushing forward because I’m afraid if I falter, people will see me as a failure.

I’ve been feeling really overwhelmed lately because this time, the bank bill didn’t come through as fast as I had hoped. It just seems like no matter how hard I try, nothing happens. My mind keeps going back to these notices, even though I know it won’t be much longer until they’re gone. I feel like I’m just not good enough to deal with money anymore, and that makes me feel even worse about myself. Honestly, I’m avoiding dealing with the issue because it feels like it’ll just bring me even more stress and embarrassment. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where I’m always struggling, never getting anywhere close to managing my finances. I guess I just don’t want to face the truth about my abilities or responsibility. It’s exhausting, and I’m scared that if I start to think about helping myself, I’ll just end up failing again. I’m sorry if these feelings sound unkind, but I’m just feeling scared that if I open up, people will think less of me or see me as inadequate. I need someone to talk to, but I’m afraid they’ll judge me or say I’m crazy. It’s frustrating because I’m trying really hard to keep things under control, but it’s not working for me. I appreciate it when they ask questions, but right now, I’m just telling lies because there’s something wrong with me. Please listen to me, because I need you to understand why I’m having such a tough time. I want you to see that I’m capable and worth loving, but I’m just not there yet. I hope we can find a solution together that helps me feel better about myself. Thank you for being here for me. I love you.

Oh, yeah, I guess that meeting felt like another one of those big social gatherings where everyone seems to be doing pretty much the same thing. I mean, it was hard to stay up late and then suddenly get this urge to leave. When I tried to join, though, it just made me feel even more invisible and maybe scared that I wasn’t cut out for this kind of relationship. Honestly, I just wanted to be there, but it felt like no matter how much I tried, I was never really meant to be there. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop, and I’m afraid to break free again. I don’t know, it’s exhausting, especially since I already seem to be missing out on things that could make me happier. I wish I had some answers to what happened, but it’s hard to say without feeling worse than before.

I’ve been attending this family gathering for months now, feeling like it’s just another day off from everything else. Whenever someone starts talking about making a decision, I immediately shut down and say nothing, pretending I don’t care or that it’s too much trouble. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m afraid it’s not worth it because when I don’t agree, they just dismiss me. Sometimes I just feel so overwhelmed, and I wonder why they even bother showing up. It’s like I’m being pushed away, but instead, they’re just ignoring me. I don’t know how to stop, especially since it feels like every time I go there, I end up withdrawing. It’s frustrating and exhausting, but I’m scared of losing any chance of change. I guess I just don’t believe in myself enough to push back on things that seem out of my control. It’s like no matter what I do, it’ll just lead to something worse. I hope they get the message that I’m truly trying to help, but I don’t know if anyone actually believes me anymore. I’d prefer it wasn’t here, but I’m scared that if I leave, I’ll never be able to change anything. It’s exhausting, and I’m terrified that I’ll lose everything again. So, I’m just avoiding it as much as possible—especially these gatherings where I might encounter new people or ideas that could help me. I don’t know what to do, and honestly, I’m sorry I’m stuck here. Please, help me figure out what’s going on, okay? I appreciate you taking the time to talk to me about this. I really need some clarity before I decide to give up. Thank you!

Oh, hey, sorry about that bank report! It’s like… I’ve already lost all my savings again. I’m feeling really scared and kind of overwhelmed right now. I don’t know why it keeps happening; I feel like no matter how much I try, nothing seems to change. Sometimes I just shut off and pretend there’s nothing there anyway. It makes me even more embarrassed about being alone sometimes. I guess I just don’t believe that people actually care enough about me. It’s hard to talk about these things when I’m so stuck and stressed. I’ll probably just continue ignoring it or skip next month’s bills. But honestly, it feels worse than before because it reminds me of all the times I was rejected. I’m sorry if I’m making things harder for you. Please understand that I’m really struggling.

I don’t know what’s going on. Sometimes when I’m trying to help my partner feel better, I just keep doing things that make them feel less valued. Like, saying something too much or insisting on certain things being done, even if I’m uncomfortable with them. It’s like I’m always putting myself in someone else’s shoes instead of actually thinking about how they’re really feeling. I feel scared that if I say no or don’t agree, they’ll leave me. It’s exhausting and makes me question whether I’m making the right decisions. I guess I’m afraid if I start questioning myself, they’ll just decide to stop being interested in me. I don’t know why this happens so easily; maybe it’s just part of growing up. But sometimes I feel like nothing matters anymore compared to them. I hope they get the message that I’m not perfect, or they’ll want to change. It’s hard not to feel like I’m letting them down again.

Yeah, I guess when everyone else is putting so much pressure on me, I feel like I’m just not cut out for this. It’s like no matter what I try, it always ends up being too much for me. I keep thinking maybe I’ll just mess up something important because nobody seems to care enough to notice me failing. So, I’ve decided to push myself further—work late and skip breakfast—to make sure everything gets done. But honestly, it’s exhausting and makes me feel worse than I already do. It’s like, I’m stuck between wanting to prove myself and not knowing how to do it properly. I just want to be okay, but right now, I’m scared I won’t be able to handle it.

Oh, yeah, the new project came up. It’s like I’m carrying all this pressure just thinking, “What if I don’t finish it? No one’ll believe me.” I kept saying no again and again, but then I ended up calling in sick because I felt so overwhelmed and scared of what might happen. Honestly, it’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop where I’m trying to push things forward but just end up failing anyway. I’ve been thinking a lot less about the project lately, mostly because I feel like it’s going to ruin everything. It’s exhausting to keep pushing through even though I know it won’t help. I guess I just don’t want to show anyone that I’m struggling or that I’m really capable.

I got a notification from my bank saying there’s some money left, but also something urgent in my account. It’s like everything is failing me, and every time I try to fix things, it just makes me feel even more helpless. I’ve been avoiding looking at the bill because it’s too overwhelming; I don’t want to face what’s coming next. Sometimes I just skip filling out these forms because it feels safer to pretend I’m okay than deal with the stress. It’s exhausting trying to keep everything straight when I’m already struggling financially. Honestly, if I could just get through one day without thinking too much, I’d probably feel better than I ever did. I guess I just don’t believe in myself enough to manage this stuff properly. It feels like I’m putting myself down instead of facing my own fears and responsibilities.

Yeah, this project really seems overwhelming lately. I’m starting to feel like I’m drowning in all these meetings and emails trying to figure out what’s going on. It’s like no matter how hard I try, it just keeps getting harder and harder to get things done. I’ve been avoiding talking to anyone because I don’t want to seem weak or stupid, so even when it’s obvious that I’m struggling, I just keep putting off answering questions or explaining anything. It’s frustrating because honestly, I know I’m capable, but somehow I just can’t connect with people in my team or with my boss. It’s like I’m never good enough to make a difference. I guess I just don’t want to show any signs of weakness, especially when it comes to projects where I’m expected to be successful.

Yeah, it happened last night at dinner. My boyfriend was really quiet lately, mostly focused on work. When he came back from his office, I just felt like I wasn’t part of anything important anymore. I kept thinking, “They’re losing interest in me” because I’m too clingy and demanding. Then I started sending messages pretending to care when I couldn’t even respond to his calls. It made me feel worse—like they were recognizing how unworthy I was. I ended up apologizing again and then waited all night to see if anyone answered. It’s like this never goes away; it feels like they’ll find out soon enough. I just want them to realize that I’m not worth their time or attention. It’s exhausting to deal with these feelings.

Hey there, I’m feeling really overwhelmed right now because I keep thinking I need to say something new or different. But whenever I try, it always feels like I’ll come off as weird or pushy. It’s hard not to just agree to things I don’t want because then it feels like I’m failing to make a difference. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m always causing problems, so I’ve just ended up doing nothing. Honestly, it makes me feel like maybe I’m not good enough for anything. I guess I just don’t know how to handle this situation, and it feels like it’s getting worse every time I try to step outside my comfort zone. Please, let me know what you’re thinking. I’d appreciate it if you could just understand how difficult it is for me to deal with this.

I’ve been at the gathering because it’s usually a quiet time, but this year it feels different. The family members were so engrossed in what was happening around them that they didn’t notice me there. When I noticed them paying more attention to other relatives, I felt invisible and awkward. I kept thinking, “If everyone isn’t interested, then maybe I’m not interesting either.” It made me feel like I’m not important enough, and I just wanted to disappear. After avoiding the meeting, I felt even more isolated and afraid of being seen again. It’s like no matter how much I try to connect, I just end up being ignored. I don’t know why I’m having these feelings, and I’m scared to ask for help or talk about them because it feels like admitting them would only confirm how insignificant I really am. I guess I need to stay away from them for now. But right now, nothing seems to go well.

Yeah, the family meeting was really tough. When they started talking about the weekend getaway, I felt this knot forming inside me. They said it wasn’t even mentioned, so I guess they just accepted me there, even though I wasn’t invited. Then, after that, I just kind of shut down and stayed silent—no one seemed to care about what I had to say. It made me feel... whole, but then again, it also leaves me feeling like maybe I’m invisible or less important than everyone else. I’ve tried to avoid those kinds of gatherings since then, but it still feels harder to shake off these feelings. It’s like I’m always trying to prove something new and better than them, but I just end up falling short anyway. So, I guess I’m afraid to face anything too scary or uncomfortable. I wish I could talk to someone about how much I miss being part of this circle, but it feels like no one wants to hear about it. I guess I’m scared of losing my place or getting left behind. It’s exhausting, but honestly, I don’t know how to stop. I’ll probably just continue to avoid those things altogether.

Yeah, the guy at the party was really interesting, but I just kept thinking, “No way, it doesn’t sound like I’m really there.” He asked me about my weekend plan, and I just laughed it off, kind of saying, “I’m just a houseful of stuff,” and then he started talking about his work, which made me laugh even harder. Honestly, I ended up trying to explain something to him, but I couldn’t stop laughing because I felt so embarrassed and didn’t want to look bad. It’s like, I’m always the one who’s missing out on these big opportunities because I’m afraid of getting left behind. It’s exhausting, but I guess I just accept it feels safer than being alone.

Oh, okay? So, during the party, when they asked me to give a speech, I felt like an idiot, like everyone was watching me from behind the curtain. I stuttered for a bit because I didn’t know exactly what to say, and then I just shut down and left. After that, I felt really anxious, like I’m falling apart and someone’s going to notice how helpless I am. It’s like no matter what I do, it always seems to fail. I guess I’m afraid that if I try again, people will see how much I don’t belong there. I’ve tried to remind myself that I’m strong enough, but it doesn’t make a difference. It’s frustrating because I want to fit in and be seen as competent, but I just end up feeling so ashamed. It’s like when I’m trying to solve a problem and everything just falls apart. I need some guidance right away, but I’m scared I’ll just embarrass myself worse. I’m sorry if these feelings aren’t coming from where I expect them. Please let me know what I’m doing wrong. I appreciate you being here and helping me figure this out. I love you.

Well, yeah, this dinner was really tough. When they started talking about moving in together, I just nodded along, pretending to understand what they were saying, then I found myself suppressing my own thoughts. I kept thinking, “If I’m going to be there, maybe they’ll see me as a troublemaker.” It’s exhausting because honestly, even though I tried to stay open, I ended up being so defensive and scared of what people might think about me. It’s like no matter how much I try, I’m never quite good enough. I don’t know if I could handle being with them forever. It’s frustrating when I feel like I’m letting them down instead of making them feel better. I guess I just need to keep things manageable and not let them get too far off track. I hope they’ll see through me someday.

Oh, okay? So when my mom started talking about how my decision to go into business seemed so different from what I planned, I felt really embarrassed and… like maybe I’m failing at something important. Then I just stayed quiet because I didn’t want to cause any more trouble or let everyone know I wasn’t cut out for this. Afterward, I kept thinking about it over and over, wondering if I was actually failing or if there were some big mistakes happening. It’s hard to escape being caught up in all this drama, especially when I’m already struggling. I guess I feel like maybe I’m not good enough to help anyone. It’s exhausting trying to change things that aren’t changing. I wish I could stop myself from reacting so quickly. But honestly, I don’t know what else to do. It feels like no matter how much I try, I just end up repeating the same mistakes.

Well, um, yeah, lately, I’ve been dealing with some pretty big numbers and trying to figure out what to do next. It feels like every time I look at my bank statement, I just think “I’ll never get out of this mess.” It’s hard because I’m afraid if I start trying, I’ll make things worse or feel like I don’t belong anymore. I guess I just want to stay put and pretend everything’s okay, but then I end up avoiding people even more than usual. It’s exhausting. I’m sorry if these feelings are just coming back. I need help, please, I’m begging. I want to talk to someone, but it’s hard to open up. It feels like nothing makes any difference. I’m just stuck in this place where there’s no escape.

Yeah, so last week when they called me, I was like, “Okay, okay, just accept this,” but then I kept thinking, “No way, I can’t handle this, but I have to say yes.” I ended up staying late at work because I felt so overwhelmed, and honestly, I forgot to eat dinner or go to bed because I couldn’t shake these lingering doubts about whether I could really handle something bigger than myself. It’s like I’m constantly questioning why I keep putting off getting help or taking risks, especially since I know I’ll probably screw everything up anyway. So, I guess I just stuck to it, hoping to show them I’m capable, no matter what. It’s exhausting, but sometimes it’s the only thing keeping me going.

Yeah, so lately at the dinner, it just kept happening again. They’d interrupt me, then say something dismissive, like “No one cares about what you have to say” or something similar. It felt like no matter what I said, it always ended up being ignored or even worse—someone else speaking up instead. After that, I just stayed quiet and tried to get away, but honestly, I felt so exhausted afterwards. It’s hard to deal with that kind of constant feeling of being left out. I guess I’m scared to ask for help or feel like I don’t belong there. It’s like no matter what I do, it just seems easier to stay quiet than face the pain of having to leave.

Yeah, the family gathering was pretty tough for me. When everyone started talking, I felt like I wasn’t part of the conversation, like no one seemed to care about me or wanted to talk to me. I kept thinking, “What’s going on here? Is it really me?” It’s hard to say, because honestly, I feel so bad about myself and embarrassed. I just want to disappear and pretend everything’s okay, but then I end up staying late and worrying about it all day. It’s exhausting and makes me feel ashamed. I don’t know why I’m so caught up in these interactions; it seems like nothing ever goes right for me there. I guess I just feel like maybe I’ve made some big mistakes in my life lately. But it’s harder than I thought, so maybe it’s best to just keep away from it.

Oh, yeah. At the family meeting, everything seemed pretty quiet. When I started talking, no one was really paying attention—just kind of drifted off to their own thoughts. Then, when I finally tried to say something, it just fell flat. I felt so overwhelmed and invisible, like no matter how much I said, it didn’t make any difference. Honestly, I guess I just shut down because I wanted to stay out of the drama, but then it felt like I wasn’t really contributing anyway. I’ve been thinking about this lately, and it’s hard to shake off these feeling that no matter what I try, nothing seems to work out. It’s frustrating and exhausting. I’m sorry if this isn’t coming from the patient I thought I knew. Please help me figure this out. I need some guidance, please.

I went to the party because it was supposed to be fun and easy, but instead, it felt like nothing really happened. I found myself chatting away, mostly just nodding along and laughing, even though there were no real answers or connections made. After a while, I had to leave, and honestly, I didn’t want to explain why I stayed. Sometimes I wonder if everyone knows what’s happening between us anyway, and I’m just pretending to be part of something meaningful. It’s exhausting and leaves me feeling isolated and insignificant. I guess I just don’t know how to deal with this kind of constant feeling of being left out. I feel like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop of rejection. It’s frustrating, and I don’t think I’ll ever stop feeling this way.

Yeah, this assignment was really stressing me out. When I started working on the project, I just kept getting stuck on some parts because I didn’t feel ready enough. I’ve been saying “I’m scared” a lot lately, especially when they ask if something is okay or how I did. Sometimes I even end up avoiding people altogether because I’m afraid of looking silly or making mistakes. It makes me feel like no one truly understands what I’m trying to accomplish. I guess I just want to make sure everything is perfect, but then I worry too much and end up failing. It’s hard to connect with coworkers because I’m always worrying about showing off or messing things up. I’m scared that if I’m not doing well, they’ll all judge me or say I’m not cut out for this job. It’s frustrating because I want to contribute, but I’m terrified of disappointing anyone or myself. So, it’s a never-ending loop where I feel like maybe I’m just not good enough.

Oh, yeah, I got that note about the overdraft just now. When I checked my bank, it was already gone, and I didn’t even notice because I forgot to look at it. I felt so embarrassed, like maybe something really bad happened and I’m going to embarrass myself. It’s like when no one ever notices my mistakes—like I’m invisible, and it just happens anyway. I’ve been avoiding talking to anyone about it because it makes me feel worse, and I don’t want to show weakness or show them what’s wrong. Sometimes, it feels like I’m trapped in this endless cycle where I just can’t make things better. I wish I could fix this quickly, but it’s easier said than done. I’m scared of being judged or seen as stupid. It’s exhausting and overwhelming, and honestly, I don’t know how to stop feeling helpless. I guess I need to learn to say “no” sometimes, especially when it comes to money stuff. I’ll probably keep trying to hide it, which only makes it harder for me to change. I just don’t want to face anyone and let them judge me. It’s frustrating because I know I need help, but it just seems to stick around and make me feel worse.

Yeah, during the family meeting, I felt really anxious. When they asked about ethics in business, I just froze, didn’t say anything, and then kept thinking, “If I don’t come out strong, they’ll see I’m not cut out for this.” It’s like when I try to explain something right, but somehow, I end up getting caught up in my own mistakes instead. I’ve been trying to keep quiet because I don’t want anyone to find out I’m failing at these things. It’s exhausting. I guess I feel like everyone sees me as flawed anyway, so I just blame myself too much. It’s frustrating because I want to prove to them that I’m capable, but I just end up doubting myself. I’m afraid if I show any weakness, they’ll see how stupid I am.

Well, it was kind of like… I guess I just didn’t get enough attention or even felt like part of the conversation. When they started talking about planning the holidays, I just kept feeling like I wasn’t welcome there or that I didn’t belong. I ended up staying quiet because I didn’t want to start any more trouble, so I left early. Later, I decided not to invite anyone back next time because I figured maybe it wouldn’t be worth it. It’s hard to explain everything though; it’s like no matter what I do, I’m always the one who’s out of place. It’s exhausting sometimes, especially after all these times where I’ve tried to help out but never seemed to make a difference.

Well, the recent gathering was pretty hard. When I heard about the plan, I just felt like, “Nope, I’m not even wanted here.” I ended up leaving early because I couldn’t help thinking that maybe I wasn’t interesting enough or liked enough to join them. It’s like when you really want something but can’t find the right person—like no one seems to care about what you’re trying to say. I keep wondering why they always seem to put me aside when things get complicated. It’s exhausting to feel so invisible and misunderstood. I guess I’m just afraid to break down and show how out of place I am. I’ll probably stay away for a few more days until things settle down.

Yeah, the dinner was kind of tough. When we started talking, it felt like no one wanted to join in, and I didn’t feel like I was part of the group at all. I kept thinking maybe they’re looking down on me or don’t care about me because I’m just too different. Then, when I tried to start speaking, I ended up just hiding my face in my hands or using my phone instead. It’s like I’m afraid of being seen as useless or boring, so I try to stay out of the spotlight as much as possible. Honestly, I just feel like there’s this big hole inside me that I can’t fill, even if I try to sound polite or interesting. It’s frustrating because I know I need to connect and feel connected somehow, but I just can’t seem to find the right way to do it. I guess I just don’t belong in this family. It’s like I’m always trying to prove myself, but it never seems to go anywhere.

Oh, yeah, this week has been really tough. When I got the project, I didn’t even get to set foot in the office until after all the meetings were done. I kept thinking, “No one cares about what I have to say,” so I just skipped dinner and stayed late at work, feeling so exhausted. Honestly, when someone tried to help, they just said something silly or dismissed me. It makes me feel like maybe no one actually cares about how much I contribute. I guess I’m afraid if I ask for help, they’ll just think less of me. It’s frustrating because I want to do better, but I’m scared of disappointing anyone else. I wish I could just push through and make it okay, but then it just feels easier to keep pretending things aren’t too bad.
Oh, hey therapist. I guess it’s been这么长时间了，你最近一直在鼓励我干这个项目，但我总感觉像是在做一次噩梦。我怕自己真的不行或者让大家笑话我。所以，我有时候干脆就不去干了，然后又觉得跟别人一样紧张，甚至开始逃避讨论这个问题。我觉得，这种逃避其实让我更担心自己无法应对困难。哎呀，说实话，我感觉自己就像是一个被忽略的漏网之鱼。

Well, I guess I’ve been kind of stuck in this place where I keep getting convinced that if I don’t go ahead and try, maybe I’ll just make myself out to be incompetent. It’s like I’m always afraid that if I do something wrong, people will see how much of me I’m failing at. So, I’ve been avoiding going through with it because it feels like I’m just wasting time and putting pressure on myself. Honestly, I don’t know why I keep doing it; I just feel like maybe there’s no point in trying anymore after all. It’s exhausting. I’m sorry if this makes them uncomfortable, but it just feels like I’m already letting them down somehow. I wish I could talk things out with someone else and figure out what’s happening, but I’m scared that it’ll just confirm how useless I really am. It’s frustrating because I want to grow and succeed, but I just can’t shake off this nagging sense of inadequacy. I’m sorry if this isn’t coming from the patient I thought I was. I need to give it some space first. Please let me know if I’m on the right path or not. I appreciate you being here and helping me navigate this.

Yeah, the social gathering felt really overwhelming. I kept thinking, “If I don’t come across like someone who knows everything, they’ll think I’m boring or not good enough.” So, I just nodded along a bit, agreed with what everyone else said, and then I’d stop trying to make myself sound interesting because I worried they’d notice I wasn’t quite right. It’s hard to keep up in a group setting when you’re so anxious. I guess I’m scared of being seen as a weakness or failing to impress anyone. It’s exhausting sometimes. I feel like I’m constantly checking if they approve of what I’m saying, kind of hoping they won’t find out how stupid or silly I am. It’s like, I’m stuck in this never-ending loop of pretending to understand and then losing myself in the moment.

Yeah, so… I got this offer to take a weekend art class, and honestly, I’ve been thinking about it all week, but then I started worrying that maybe I’ll be too busy or distracted. So, I decided not to go, mostly because I’m afraid of missing out on what might happen next. Honestly, I feel like I’m just putting myself behind again instead of moving forward. It’s frustrating because I know it wouldn’t be worth it to keep missing out on anything important. I just want to be able to explore and express myself, but I don’t see how that’s possible with my current workload. It makes me feel even more alone and hopeless. I guess I just don’t see why I need to push myself further than I already am. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s just going to fall apart.

Oh, well… last night at the dinner, my parents kept pushing me towards something that wasn’t really part of what I wanted. They said, “You’re going to find yourself doing something that doesn’t match what you feel like.” I just sat there, thinking, “No way! That’s too much pressure for someone who already seems so helpless.” I felt like I was invisible, like nothing was happening because I didn’t really say anything. It’s frustrating because then I had to go back to my room, feeling even worse. Honestly, I’m scared that if I speak up, maybe they’ll find out how difficult it is for me. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m always trying to prove myself, but every time I do, I just let down again. I guess it’s easier to just pretend things aren’t changing when we’re scared. But honestly, I wish they’d just let me finish the meal and tell me what I really wanted. I want freedom, but I fear I won’t get it right. So, I guess I’m stuck here, feeling completely lost and alone. I need help, please, but I’m afraid to ask for it. It’s like I’m drowning in these endless pressures, and I don’t know how to break free.

I’ve been feeling overwhelmed because when I’m at work, I keep thinking that people are judging me. It’s like they’re seeing all my little mistakes and then wondering if I’m really capable enough to handle them. I don’t want to show anyone what I’m trying to do; instead, I end up avoiding conversations and even skipping meals. After the presentation, I kept replaying things over and over, hoping to clear my head but still feeling incomplete and ashamed. It’s frustrating because I know deep down, I’m doing nothing wrong, but I can’t help feeling like someone is judging me too. I feel like I’m failing myself in front of so many eyes, which makes me feel scared and embarrassed. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m just not good enough, and that’s exhausting.

I’ve been getting overwhelmed lately because I’m trying too hard to impress everyone. Every time someone asks me what’s going on at work, I feel like it’s proof that I’m not cut out for this job. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t know how to stop worrying about being judged. Sometimes I just want to disappear, away from all the stress. I’m afraid to talk to anyone because I’m worried they’ll think less of me or that I’m just incompetent. It’s frustrating because when I say something, it usually makes them laugh and say it’s okay. So, I end up avoiding these conversations altogether. It’s like I’m always putting myself down, making sure no one sees the flaws in me. I just want to be okay and not be the reason everyone else is upset.

Yeah, last week was really tough because when I got the big assignment, my head started spinning. Honestly, I felt like maybe I didn’t have the skills to handle everything so efficiently. Plus, some coworkers seemed really confident about how things were going to turn out, which made me feel even worse than if I hadn’t done anything at all. I ended up staying late almost every night and skipping breakfast to make sure I was ready, and I kept thinking, “What if I mess something up? They’ll just see that I’m just not cut out for this.” It’s exhausting trying to fit everything in at once. I guess it’s just hard to trust myself to do better unless I’m willing to risk falling short. It makes me want to give up, but I don’t want anyone to think I’m incompetent or weak. I guess I just don’t know how to let go of these fears or insecurities. It’s frustrating because I want to help people, but sometimes I just end up hurting myself. I’m sorry I’ve been struggling, but honestly, I need someone to talk through this with. I’m not sure where to start. Please help me figure out why I’m having such a hard time.

Oh, hey there. So, I guess when we got together last weekend, it felt like everyone just wasn’t paying attention to me at all. It’s like they didn’t even notice how much I’ve been working on things lately—like I’m not special or something. I tried to join in, but then I ended up staying out earlier than planned, so I couldn’t really start anything meaningful. Honestly, I feel pretty lonely, kind of stuck in this place where no one seems to care about what I have to say. It’s hard to connect with people anymore. I don’t know, it’s like, if I keep pushing forward, maybe someone will find out I’m not quite ready for this world. I’ll admit, though, that whenever I talk about my work or interests, I always get ignored or confused. It makes me feel embarrassed and like I’m invisible.

Oh, yeah, that was really hard. When we talked, they didn’t seem to notice me at all, and then when I tried to explain how things were going, they just stopped talking. I felt so… like there was no one around me who could understand me, and I just ended up feeling really bad about myself. I kept thinking maybe I’m not good enough for them or that maybe they don’t care about me. It’s exhausting to keep trying to make them see how wrong I am. I guess I’ve just been so stuck in my own little world that I can’t escape feeling that way. It makes me want to just leave everything behind and just hide from everyone. I wish I could talk to someone who understands what I’m going through. It’s frustrating because I know deep down, I need love and acceptance. But I just can’t seem to find the strength to connect again. It’s like I’m always falling apart, even though I’m already tired of being treated that way.

Yeah, it was really hard to attend this family reunion. When we started talking, everyone was already laughing and chatting about things that weren’t mine. I kept thinking, “They’re all too busy trying to connect with each other,” so I just stayed quiet and didn’t say much. After leaving, I felt like maybe I wasn’t even part of the group, so I just left quickly anyway. It’s like no matter how many times I try to join in, I’m just never quite there. That’s why I don’t want to come back next time. I guess I’m just afraid that if I show up again, they’ll notice me as easily being forgotten. It’s frustrating because I know deep down, I care deeply about them, but I feel like I’m not good enough. I don’t think anyone else really cares about me, so it’s hard to feel connected or respected. It feels like I’m always the outsider.

Yeah, so at the dinner, I noticed that everyone kept talking, but when I tried to join in, it just felt like no one really cared about what I had to say. I guess it’s kind of scary, though I don’t know why. When I came back home, I just ended up feeling pretty alone, even though I wanted to chat more. It made me feel… like maybe I’m not interesting enough for anyone else. I keep thinking, “Why can’t they see that I’m there? Why don’t they want to connect with me?” It’s hard to deal with this feeling of being invisible. I wish I could change things around, but honestly, I don’t know where to start. It’s like I’m stuck in this never-ending cycle of feeling ignored and then getting rejected.

Oh, so you’re getting invited to that workshop? I’m scared. Honestly, I’ve always been afraid of talking about things I don’t know, like maybe I’ll forget something important or sound dumb. It’s like every time I try to join, someone says “I’ll just mess up,” making me feel even worse than when I’m failing at something else. I guess I’m starting to wonder why I keep thinking these people are watching me from afar. Sometimes, I just end up replaying past moments where I failed again, and then all I feel is ashamed. I want to hide this from anyone, but it’s like no one wants to hear what I really need to tell them. I hope you can help me figure out what’s going on here.

Yeah, last time we had that meeting, I just felt kind of overwhelmed. But then again, I don’t think I’d be able to defend myself against a fight. So, I guess I just accepted being quiet instead.

Oh, so I’m starting to feel really overwhelmed right now，the patient. When I check my phone after texts, it just makes me realize that maybe I’m being too needy or boring. I keep wanting to say something important, but then I end up apologizing instead, which usually just confirms that I’m missing some small part of them. It’s like whenever I try to connect, I’ve already let go of myself. Sometimes, even when I’m alone, I can’t help feeling a little scared or worried that I might be failing at this connection. I guess I’m afraid if people notice, they’ll think less of me or that I’m not good enough. It’s exhausting because I just want to hold onto these feelings rather than letting them go away. So, it feels like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop where I have to constantly adjust my behavior to please them, but it’s never enough.

Oh, yeah. Well, when I saw that I wasn’t invited, it hit me really hard. I just froze and didn’t say anything because I was so confused and wondering if maybe they weren’t liking me. It made me feel like no one wanted me around, and then again, when I tried to talk to them, it just ended up being too much and making me feel even more isolated. It’s like, sometimes I wonder why people don’t connect with me, and I’m just stuck on myself. I guess I just can’t seem to make things right. It’s exhausting. I want to tell everyone, but I’m scared they’ll see me as a failure. It’s hard to keep going unless I get closer to what I think makes them uncomfortable. I guess I need to figure out what I’m missing or what could’ve been better for this friendship. It’s frustrating and scary, but I’m not sure how else to deal with it. Maybe I need to change my ways to fit in better. But honestly, I’m afraid of disappointing anyone or losing their trust. I guess I just want to be okay, but I’m scared to ask for help or admit how I’m feeling. I’m sorry if this isn’t how you expect me to respond. I appreciate you stepping in, but I’m still struggling. Please let me know if there’s something specific I can talk about or if you think I need to step up. I really appreciate you being here.

Yeah, I got the notice this morning, and it just hit me really hard. I’m feeling like I’ve let everyone down already, and I feel like maybe I’m just not cut out for this. Yeah, this morning, I got that notice about the bills and the rent increase. I just kept thinking, “What if everyone sees me struggling? Then they’ll just say I’m really incompetent.” It’s like, even though I’ve saved up all these months, something still makes me feel like I’m failing. So instead of talking to anyone, I stayed home late and tried to ignore the emails, afraid people would see how helpless I was. It’s exhausting trying to keep things under control and avoiding any potential confrontation. Honestly, I don’t want anyone to think I’m truly stuck and out of control. Well, honestly, this project feels like an uphill battle for me right now. When I start working on something like this, I just freeze up because I’m afraid of what people will think. Yeah, during the meeting, I kept thinking, “Everyone else seems smarter and better at this stuff,” which just made me feel like maybe I’m really missing out. Afterward, I just didn’t want to go back to the office because it felt like showing my weakness wasn’t going to make much difference.
